sourceQuestion:
At a particular time of computation, the value of a counting semaphore is 7. Then 20 P operations and 15 V operations were completed on this semaphore. The resulting value of the semaphore is:
A. 42
B. 2
C. 7
D. 12

parsed.stem: At a particular time of computation, the value of a counting semaphore is 7. Then 20 P operations and 15 V operations were completed on this semaphore. The resulting value of the semaphore is:

sourceQuestion:
A counting semaphore was initialized to 10. Then 6 P (wait) operations and 4 V (signal) operations were completed on this semaphore. The resulting value of the semaphore is:
A. 0
B. 8
C. 10
D. 12

parsed.stem: A counting semaphore was initialized to 10. Then 6 P (wait) operations and 4 V (signal) operations were completed on this semaphore. The resulting value of the semaphore is:

sourceAnswer: A. It ensures that no process executes CODE SECTION Q before every process has finished CODE SECTION P.

options.text: It ensures that no process executes CODE SECTION Q before every process has finished CODE SECTION P.
It ensures that at most n-1 processes are in CODE SECTION Q at any time.
It ensures that all processes execute CODE SECTION Q concurrently.
It ensures that at most n-1 processes are in CODE SECTION P at any time.

sourceQuestion:
Processes P1, P2, P3, P4 arrive in that order at times 0, 1, 2, and 8 milliseconds respectively, and have execution times of 10, 13, 2, and 9 milliseconds respectively. Shortest Remaining Time First (SRTF) algorithm is used as the CPU scheduling policy. Ignore context switching times.

Which ONE of the following correctly gives the average turnaround time of the four processes in milliseconds?

A. 22
B. 15
C. 37
D. 19

parsed.stem:
Processes P1, P2, P3, P4 arrive in that order at times 0, 1, 2, and 8 milliseconds respectively, and have execution times of 10, 13, 2, and 9 milliseconds respectively. Shortest Remaining Time First (SRTF) algorithm is used as the CPU scheduling policy. Ignore context switching times.

Which ONE of the following correctly gives the average turnaround time of the four processes in milliseconds?

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following standard library functions will ALWAYS invoke a system call when executed from a single-threaded process in a UNIX/Linux operating system?
A. exit
B. malloc
C. sleep
D. strlen

parsed.stem: Which of the following standard library functions will ALWAYS invoke a system call when executed from a single-threaded process in a UNIX/Linux operating system?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following statements with respect to user-level threads and kernel-supported threads:
I. Context switch is faster with kernel-supported threads.
II. For user-level threads, a system call can block the entire process.
III. Kernel supported threads can be scheduled independently.
IV. User level threads are transparent to the kernel.
Which of the above statements are true?
A. (I), (II) and (IV) only
B. (II) and (III) only
C. (I) and (III) only
D. (II), (III) and (IV) only

parsed.stem:
Consider the following statements with respect to user-level threads and kernel-supported threads:
I. Context switch is faster with kernel-supported threads.
II. For user-level threads, a system call can block the entire process.
III. Kernel supported threads can be scheduled independently.
IV. User level threads are transparent to the kernel.
Which of the above statements are true?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following statements about user level threads and kernel level threads. Which one of the following statements is FALSE?
A. Context switch time is longer for kernel level threads than for user level threads.
B. User level threads do not need any hardware support.
C. Related kernel level threads can be scheduled on different processors in a multi-processor system.
D. Kernel level threads cannot share the code segment.
sourceAnswer: D. Kernel level threads cannot share the code segment.

parsed.stem: Consider the following statements about user level threads and kernel level threads. Which one of the following statements is FALSE?

options.text: Context switch time is longer for kernel level threads than for user level threads.
User level threads do not need any hardware support.
Related kernel level threads can be scheduled on different processors in a multi-processor system.
Kernel level threads cannot share the code segment.

sourceQuestion:
A thread is usually defined as a "light weight process" because an Operating System (OS) maintains smaller data structure for a thread than for a process. In relation to this, which of the following statement is TRUE?
A. On per-thread basis, the OS maintains only CPU register state.
B. The OS does not maintain a separate stack for each thread.
C. On per-thread basis, the OS does not maintain virtual memory state.
D. On per-thread basis, the OS does not maintain scheduling state.
sourceAnswer: C. On per-thread basis, the OS does not maintain virtual memory state.

parsed.stem: A thread is usually defined as a "light weight process" because an Operating System (OS) maintains smaller data structure for a thread than for a process. In relation to this, which of the following statement is TRUE?

options.text: On per-thread basis, the OS maintains only CPU register state.
The OS does not maintain a separate stack for each thread.
On per-thread basis, the OS does not maintain virtual memory state.
On per-thread basis, the OS does not maintain scheduling state.

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following is FALSE?
A. User level threads are not scheduled by the kernel.
B. When a user level thread is blocked, all other threads of its process are blocked.
C. Context switching between user level threads is faster than context switching between kernel level threads.
D. Kernel level threads do not share the code segment of the processes to which they belong.
sourceAnswer: D. Kernel level threads do not share the code segment of the processes to which they belong.

parsed.stem: Which one of the following is FALSE?

options.text: User level threads are not scheduled by the kernel.
When a user level thread is blocked, all other threads of its process are blocked.
Context switching between user level threads is faster than context switching between kernel level threads.
Kernel level threads do not share the code segment of the processes to which they belong.

sourceQuestion:
Threads of a process share
A. global variables but not heap
B. heap but not global variables
C. neither global variables nor heap
D. both heap and global variables
sourceAnswer: D. both heap and global variables

parsed.stem: Threads of a process share

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following is/are shared by all the threads in a process?
I. Program counter
II. Stack
III. Address space
IV. Registers

A. (I) and (II) only
B. (III) only
C. (IV) only
D. (III) and (IV) only

parsed.stem:
Which of the following is/are shared by all the threads in a process?
I. Program counter
II. Stack
III. Address space
IV. Registers

sourceAnswer: C. At least one of the threads will print the value of y as 2

options.text: Both T1 and T2 will print the value of x as 2
At least one of P1 and P2 will print the value of x as 4
At least one of the threads will print the value of y as 2
Both T1 and T2 will print the value of y as 1

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following statements about threads is/are TRUE?
A. Threads can only be implemented in kernel space
B. Each thread has its own file descriptor table for open files
C. All the threads belonging to a process share a common stack
D. Threads belonging to a process are by default not protected from each other
sourceAnswer: D. Threads belonging to a process are by default not protected from each other

parsed.stem: Which of the following statements about threads is/are TRUE?

options.text: Threads can only be implemented in kernel space
Each thread has its own file descriptor table for open files
All the threads belonging to a process share a common stack
Threads belonging to a process are by default not protected from each other

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following is NOT shared by the threads of the same process?

A. Stack
B. Address Space
C. File Descriptor Table
D. Message Queue

parsed.stem: Which one of the following is NOT shared by the threads of the same process?

sourceQuestion:
Match the pairs in the following:

(A) Virtual memory      (p) Temporal Locality
(B) Shared memory       (q) Spatial Locality
(C) Look-ahead buffer   (r) Address Translation
(D) Lookaside buffer    (s) Mutual Exclusion

A. A-r, B-s, C-q, D-p
B. A-r, B-s, C-p, D-q
C. A-s, B-r, C-p, D-q
D. A-r, B-p, C-s, D-q

parsed.stem: Match the pairs in the following:

options.text: Lookaside buffer (s) Mutual Exclusion A. A-r, B-s, C-q, D-p B. A-r, B-s, C-p, D-q C. A-s, B-r, C-p, D-q D. A-r, B-p, C-s, D-q

sourceQuestion:
Indicate all the false statements from the statements given below:
A. The amount of virtual memory available is limited by the availability of the secondary memory.
B. Any implementation of a critical section requires the use of an indivisible machine instruction, such as test-and-set.
C. The use of monitors ensures that no dead-locks will be caused.
D. The LRU page-replacement policy may cause thrashing for some type of programs.
E. The best fit techniques for physical memory management ensures that the free memory available will never be fragmented.

parsed.stem: Indicate all the false statements from the statements given below:

options.text: The amount of virtual memory available is limited by the availability of the secondary memory.
Any implementation of a critical section requires the use of an indivisible machine instruction, such as test-and-set.
The use of monitors ensures that no dead-locks will be caused.
The LRU page-replacement policy may cause thrashing for some type of programs.
The best fit techniques for physical memory management ensures that the free memory available will never be fragmented.

sourceQuestion:
Which one of the following statements is true?
A. Macro definitions cannot appear within other macro definitions in assembly language programs
B. Overlaying is used to run a program which is longer than the address space of a computer
C. Virtual memory can be used to accommodate a program which is longer than the physical memory of a computer
D. It is not possible to write interrupt service routines in a high level language
sourceAnswer: C. Virtual memory can be used to accommodate a program which is longer than the physical memory of a computer

parsed.stem: Which one of the following statements is true?

options.text: Macro definitions cannot appear within other macro definitions in assembly language programs
Overlaying is used to run a program which is longer than the address space of a computer
Virtual memory can be used to accommodate a program which is longer than the physical memory of a computer
It is not possible to write interrupt service routines in a high level language

sourceQuestion:
In a paged segmented scheme of memory management, the segment table itself must have a page table because
A. The segment table is often too large to fit in one page
B. Each segment is spread over a number of pages
C. Segment tables point to page tables and not to the physical locations of the segments
D. The processor's description base register points to a page table
sourceAnswer: A. The segment table is often too large to fit in one page

parsed.stem: In a paged segmented scheme of memory management, the segment table itself must have a page table because

options.text: The segment table is often too large to fit in one page
Each segment is spread over a number of pages
Segment tables point to page tables and not to the physical locations of the segments
The processor's description base register points to a page table

sourceQuestion:
In a virtual memory system the address space specified by the address lines of the CPU must be _____ than the physical memory size and _____ than the secondary storage size.
A. smaller, smaller
B. smaller, larger
C. larger, smaller
D. larger, larger

parsed.stem: In a virtual memory system the address space specified by the address lines of the CPU must be _____ than the physical memory size and _____ than the secondary storage size.

sourceQuestion:
If an instruction takes i microseconds and a page fault takes an additional j microseconds, the effective instruction time if on the average a page fault occurs every k instructions is:

A. i + j/k
B. i + j*k
C. (i + j)/k
D. (i + j)*k

parsed.stem: If an instruction takes i microseconds and a page fault takes an additional j microseconds, the effective instruction time if on the average a page fault occurs every k instructions is:

sourceQuestion:
A certain computer system has the segmented paging architecture for virtual memory. The memory is byte addressable. Both virtual and physical address spaces contain 2^32 bytes each. The virtual address space is divided into 2^6 non-overlapping equal size segments. The memory management unit (MMU) has a hardware segment table. Assume that the page table of each segment is stored entirely in one page.

What is the minimum page size in bytes so that a segment requires at most one page to store its page table?

A. 2^10
B. 2^12
C. 2^14
D. 2^16

parsed.stem:
A certain computer system has the segmented paging architecture for virtual memory. The memory is byte addressable. Both virtual and physical address spaces contain 2^32 bytes each. The virtual address space is divided into 2^6 non-overlapping equal size segments. The memory management unit (MMU) has a hardware segment table. Assume that the page table of each segment is stored entirely in one page.

What is the minimum page size in bytes so that a segment requires at most one page to store its page table?

sourceQuestion:
A multi-user, multi-processing operating system cannot be implemented on hardware that does not support:

A. Address translation
B. DMA for disk transfer
C. At least two modes of CPU execution (privileged and non-privileged)
D. Demand paging
sourceAnswer: C. At least two modes of CPU execution (privileged and non-privileged)

parsed.stem: A multi-user, multi-processing operating system cannot be implemented on hardware that does not support:

options.text: At least two modes of CPU execution (privileged and non-privileged)

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following is/are advantage(s) of virtual memory?
A. Faster access to memory on an average.
B. Processes can be given protected address spaces.
C. Linker can assign addresses independent of where the program will be loaded in physical memory.
D. Program larger than the physical memory size can be run.

parsed.stem: Which of the following is/are advantage(s) of virtual memory?

options.text: Faster access to memory on an average.
Processes can be given protected address spaces.
Linker can assign addresses independent of where the program will be loaded in physical memory.
Program larger than the physical memory size can be run.

sourceQuestion:

A. 1.9999 milliseconds
B. 1 millisecond
C. 9.999 microseconds
D. 1.3999 microseconds
